preferredDeck: More accurate memory pressure and smoother large views
editorialGuidance: Keep the tone concise, professional, and specific.
Lead with user-facing outcomes, shipping milestones, trust wins, and installability improvements.
Demote internal cleanup unless it clearly changes the shipped product.
pinnedHighlights: Freed Desktop now uses macOS physical footprint for memory-pressure gates, so reclaimable WebKit RSS no longer blocks social scraping by itself
Freed Desktop now caches hot-path time formatters and keeps the Friends graph layout worker alive across resizes, so dense graph views recover instead of staying stuck on Building graph
Freed Desktop now trims oversized WebKit caches during normal memory monitoring, before social scrape preflight has to block work
Dense Map views now suppress decorative marker chrome during movement and keep rendered marker data stable while panning
Dense Friends graphs now use deterministic large-graph layout, virtualized sidebar rows, dense Pixi layers, and capped interaction redraws so 1,600-person views mount and zoom within the frame budget
WebKit NetworkCache Records now trim during social scrape memory preflight

Large-library feed search now yields more often while building its MiniSearch index and defers bulk-action ID allocation until a command runs, eliminating the 58 ms search long task in the 5,000 item perf guard.
Dense Friends graphs now allocate avatar sprites and masks only for avatars that are actually shown, avoiding thousands of unused Pixi display objects while zoomed out or when nodes have no avatar.

Large Desktop libraries now send a slimmer renderer item projection, capping feed preview text at 280 characters and keeping only content-signal tags in list state. On the local 11,801 item library this trims another measured 2.08 MB of raw item JSON before WebKit object expansion.
Google Drive now syncs through native Freed Desktop requests, avoiding renderer fetch failures that surfaced as generic Load failed errors
Google Contacts keeps using the shared Google Drive token, and Drive startup failures no longer mark Contacts auth as broken after OAuth succeeds
Google Drive API errors now preserve Google's response body details in sync diagnostics, so scope and permission failures are visible in logs and provider health
Freed Desktop rebuilds compacted feed documents to cut WebKit memory pressure
Oversized feed text compaction now rebuilds a fresh Automerge document when the saved binary is already large, so old article text history does not keep WebKit memory high after relaunch

Social scraper startup now reserves memory headroom before opening background WebKit work, reducing the chance that Facebook, Instagram, or LinkedIn scraping pushes an already heavy renderer into high pressure

When a Facebook, Instagram, or LinkedIn scrape is blocked for memory, Freed Desktop now recycles the preserved scraper WebView too, instead of leaving that hidden WebKit process alive after the blocked start
Large Desktop libraries now send compact feed text snippets to the renderer and load longer reader text on demand, reducing the item payload the main WebKit renderer has to clone and retain
Freed Desktop no longer sends synced reader text through every renderer item, trimming another measured 1.38 MB of raw item JSON from the local library before WebKit object expansion
Freed Desktop now sends even smaller feed preview snippets to the renderer, cutting another measured 14.6 MB from the local item payload before browser object expansion
Freed Desktop feed cards now keep remote media previews reader-only, avoiding eager image and video loads in the main feed while preserving full reader hydration
Dense Map views now cap rendered marker DOM work, index marker group counts, and keep 1,600-location views within the frame budget

Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn scraper windows now use provider-specific WebKit data stores, keeping social browsing heaps and caches separated from the main app renderer while preserving sign-in state
Feed-card regression coverage now protects that reader-only media policy so release validation catches accidental inline media work
Freed Desktop improves dense Map rendering and keeps Google sync on native requests
Freed Desktop trims renderer memory pressure and keeps dense Map rendering fast
Freed Desktop trims Friends graph allocations and keeps dense Map rendering fast
editorialNotes: v26.5.614-dev adds the measured 2.08 MB renderer item projection reduction from PR #475.
v26.5.615-dev adds the Friends graph lazy avatar allocation fix from PR #476.
v26.5.616-dev adds the feed search frame-budget cleanup from PR #477.
v26.5.618-dev replaces the canceled v26.5.617-dev attempt and includes PR #471, PR #472, and PR #478.
v26.5.619-dev adds the dense Friends graph mount and interaction fix from PR #482 and the dense Map interaction trim from PR #480.
v26.5.620-dev adds proactive WebKit cache trimming during regular memory monitoring from PR #483.
v26.5.621-dev adds macOS physical-footprint memory gating from PR #484.
v26.5.622-dev adds cached hot-path time formatters and Friends graph layout worker recovery from PR #485.
